rule: Meet WCAG AA contrast ratio of 4.5:1 for normal text
do_pattern: Ensure all body text has at least 4.5:1 contrast against its background — use a contrast checker before shipping
do_code: color: #1a1a1a; background: #ffffff; /* ratio 18.1:1 */
dont_pattern: Use light gray text on white backgrounds that look clean in design tools but fail for real users
dont_code: color: #aaaaaa; background: #ffffff; /* ratio 2.3:1 — fails WCAG AA */

rule: Never remove focus outlines
do_pattern: Keep visible focus indicators on all focusable elements — style them to match your brand if needed
do_code: *:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #005fcc; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: 3px; }
dont_pattern: Remove outlines globally to satisfy a designer who finds them ugly — this makes keyboard navigation invisible

rule: Respect prefers-reduced-motion
do_pattern: Wrap all animations in a media query so users who need reduced motion get a static experience
do_code: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; } }
dont_pattern: Run animations unconditionally — causes nausea and disorientation for users with vestibular disorders
dont_code: .hero { animation: slideIn 1.2s ease; } /* no reduced-motion guard */

rule: Provide skip-to-content as first focusable element
do_pattern: Add a visually hidden skip link that becomes visible on focus — saves keyboard users from tabbing through the full nav on every page
do_code: Skip to main content /* CSS: .skip-link { position: absolute; transform: translateY(-100%); } .skip-link:focus { transform: translateY(0); } */
dont_pattern: Force keyboard users to tab through the entire navigation bar on every single page load — common on nav-heavy sites

rule: Use semantic HTML before reaching for ARIA
do_pattern: Native elements like button nav main h1–h6 give you semantics keyboard support and screen reader behavior for free
do_code: <button type="button" onclick="openMenu()">Menu</button>
dont_pattern: Recreate native behavior with divs and spans — you will miss edge cases and break assistive technology

rule: Always use a visible label — never placeholder-only
do_pattern: Every input needs a persistent visible label element — placeholders disappear on focus and have poor contrast
do_code: <label for="email">Email address</label> <input id="email" type="email" autocomplete="email" />
dont_pattern: Use placeholder text as the only label — users forget what the field is for after they start typing

rule: Show inline validation errors directly below the field
do_pattern: Surface errors immediately beneath the failing field with an aria-describedby link so screen readers also get the message
do_code: Enter a valid email address
dont_pattern: Dump all errors in a single block at the top of the form — users must hunt to match each error to its field

rule: Validate on blur not only on submit
do_pattern: Trigger field-level validation when the user leaves each field so they get feedback before attempting to submit
do_code: input.addEventListener('blur', (e) => { validate(e.target); });
dont_pattern: Only validate on form submit — forces users to discover and fix multiple errors after the entire form is already filled out

rule: Use the correct input type for mobile keyboards
do_pattern: Setting type correctly triggers the right mobile keyboard and enables browser autofill — no extra code required
do_code: <input type="email" autocomplete="email" /> <input type="tel" inputmode="numeric" autocomplete="tel" /> <input type="password" autocomplete="current-password" />
dont_pattern: Use type=text for everything — users on mobile get a QWERTY keyboard for a phone number field

rule: Confirm destructive actions before executing
do_pattern: Show a confirmation dialog before any action that cannot be undone — delete cancel subscription clear cart
do_code: Permanently delete this item?
dont_pattern: Execute irreversible actions immediately on a single click with no warning or undo path
source: Nielsen Norman Group / Laws of UX

rule: Make the primary navigation sticky
do_pattern: Keep the nav accessible at all times using position sticky — users should never have to scroll back to the top to navigate
do_code: nav { position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 100; background: var(--surface); }
dont_pattern: Let navigation scroll out of view on long pages — forces disorienting scroll-back behavior

rule: Highlight the active page in the nav
do_pattern: Use aria-current=page and a visual indicator so users always know where they are in the site
do_code: Pricing /* .nav-active { font-weight: 500; border-bottom: 2px solid currentColor; } */
dont_pattern: Style all nav items identically — Baymard found 91% of sites fail to show the user's current scope in navigation

rule: Write descriptive link text
do_pattern: Link text must describe the destination on its own — screen readers list all links out of context
do_code: Read the Acme Corp case study
dont_pattern: Use generic anchor text that communicates nothing without the surrounding sentence

rule: Provide a working 404 page with navigation
do_pattern: A helpful 404 keeps users in your site — include nav a search bar and links to popular sections
do_code:
Page not found

Here are some places to go:

Home Search
dont_pattern: Serve a blank page or raw server error with no navigation — the user's only option is to leave

rule: Keep UI transition durations between 150–300ms
do_pattern: Interactions should feel instant — micro-interactions in this range confirm actions without slowing the user down
do_code: .btn { transition: background-color 180ms ease, transform 180ms ease; } .modal { transition: opacity 250ms ease-in-out; }
dont_pattern: Use 600–1000ms transitions on interactive elements — the interface feels sluggish and unresponsive

rule: Animate only transform and opacity for performance
do_pattern: These two properties are handled by the GPU compositor and never trigger layout or paint — everything else does
do_code: .card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); opacity: 0.95; transition: transform 200ms ease
dont_pattern: opacity 200ms ease; }""
dont_code: Animate width height top left margin or background-size — each frame forces the browser to recalculate the full layout
severity: .dropdown { transition: height 300ms ease; } /* triggers layout recalc every frame */

rule: Serve images in modern formats with a fallback
do_pattern: AVIF saves 50% over JPEG — use picture with a JPEG fallback for browsers that don't support it yet
dont_pattern: Serve all images as uncompressed PNG or JPEG with no size optimization

rule: Lazy load below-the-fold images
do_pattern: Native lazy loading defers off-screen images until the user scrolls near them — one attribute saves seconds of load time on media-heavy pages
dont_pattern: Eagerly load every image on the page including those 3000px below the fold on first visit

rule: Set explicit width and height on every image
do_pattern: Declaring dimensions lets the browser reserve the correct space before the image loads — prevents cumulative layout shift (CLS)
dont_pattern: Omit dimensions — layout reflowing as images load is the leading cause of poor CLS scores

rule: Defer non-critical JavaScript
do_pattern: Scripts with defer execute after HTML parsing — async third-party scripts should never block the main thread
do_code: <script src="analytics.js" defer></script> <script src="chat-widget.js" async></script>
dont_pattern: Load all scripts synchronously in the head — each one blocks the browser from rendering the page

rule: Virtualize long lists
do_pattern: Rendering only the visible rows keeps the DOM small and scroll fast — essential for lists over 200 items
do_code: // react-window example import { FixedSizeList } from 'react-window'; <FixedSizeList height={600} itemCount={10000} itemSize={48} width="100%"> {Row} </FixedSizeList>
dont_pattern: Render all 10 000 items into the DOM unconditionally — causes multi-second paint times and janky scrolling

rule: Minify and code-split production bundles
do_pattern: Build tools remove whitespace dead code and split by route so users only download what the current page needs
do_code: // vite.config.js build: { rollupOptions: { output: { manualChunks: { vendor: ['react','react-dom'] } } } }
dont_pattern: Ship a single unminified bundle containing every route and dependency regardless of which page the user is on

rule: Show a loading state for any action over 200ms
do_pattern: Users expect feedback within 200ms — show a spinner skeleton or inline loading state immediately on any async action
do_code: button.textContent = 'Saving…'; button.disabled = true; button.setAttribute('aria-busy', 'true'); await save(); button.textContent = 'Saved'; button.disabled = false;
dont_pattern: Leave the UI completely static during a 2-second API call — users assume nothing happened and click again
dont_code: No loading state — button stays unchanged during async
source: Nielsen Norman Group response time limits

rule: Design for mobile-first then enhance for desktop
do_pattern: Start with the smallest screen and add complexity — this forces content prioritization and prevents mobile as an afterthought
do_code: /* base: mobile */ .nav { display: flex; flex-direction: column; } /* enhance */ @media (min-width: 768px) { .nav { flex-direction: row; } }
dont_pattern: Design for desktop and bolt on a mobile stylesheet — the result is cramped unoptimized mobile experiences used by 60%+ of your traffic
dont_code: /* desktop first — mobile is an override */ @media (max-width: 768px) { /* cramped fixes */ }

rule: Ensure color is never the only means of conveying information
do_pattern: Always pair color with a label icon pattern or shape — 8% of men have color vision deficiency
do_code: /* Error state */ ✕ Error: Invalid email address
dont_pattern: Use red text alone to signal an error with no other differentiator — invisible to colorblind users
